preamble: The fronting shift from labial [p] to the palatal stop [c] is rare but well-documented in certain Bantu languages and specific dialectal developments in Romance languages.

examples.note: Bantu languages exhibit instances where labials become palatals under the influence of the high front glide [j].
In specific contexts, [p] shifts forward to a palatal articulation in some Southern Italian/Sicilian dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
